pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh line 62 ('echo "======================================================="' followed by 'TASK: Generate Patient Bed Cards') and export_result.sh line 11 (CHANNEL_NAME="Bed_Card_Generator"). Task description specifies: 'Channel Name: Bed_Card_Generator'.
Verified in task.json description: 'TCP Listener on port 6661'. Confirmed in export_result.sh line 41 (sends test message to 'localhost 6661'). Setup instructions echo port 6661 for the TCP listener.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 28 (mkdir -p /home/ga/bed_cards, chmod 777 /home/ga/bed_cards). Task.json description confirms: 'Destination: File Writer to /home/ga/bed_cards'.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 21 (INSERT INTO doctors (doctor_id, full_name) VALUES ('DOC202', 'Dr. Lisa Cuddy')) and export_result.sh line 25 (TEST_DOC_ID="DOC202" # Should map to Dr. Lisa Cuddy).

Verified in setup_task.sh line 21 database INSERT and export_result.sh line 25 comment. Doctor names are fictional characters from House MD TV series (2004-2012): Dr. Lisa Cuddy was the Dean of Medicine at the fictional Princeton-Plainsboro Teaching Hospital, played by Lisa Edelstein. Sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lisa_Cuddy, https://house.fandom.com/wiki/Lisa_Cuddy
Verified in export_result.sh line 22 (TEST_LAST_NAME="TESTPATIENT"). This is a clearly synthetic test patient name used for verification testing.
Verified in export_result.sh line 24 (TEST_MRN="MRN99999"). This is a clearly synthetic Medical Record Number for testing.

Task description explicitly states: 'Type: PostgreSQL' and 'Connection: jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb'. Verified in setup_task.sh lines 10-23 using 'docker exec nextgen-postgres psql' commands.
Task description states: "Table: 'doctors' (columns: doctor_id, full_name)". Verified in setup_task.sh lines 11-14 (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS doctors with doctor_id and full_name columns).

Verified by examining setup_task.sh lines 18-23. The database is populated with exactly 3 doctor records: DOC101->Dr. Gregory House, DOC202->Dr. Lisa Cuddy, DOC303->Dr. James Wilson. All are fictional characters from House MD TV series.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires creating a NextGen Connect (Mirth Connect) channel named 'Bed_Card_Generator' that processes HL7 ADT messages received on TCP port 6661, performs a database lookup of attending doctor information from a PostgreSQL table (doctors table with doctor_id and full_name columns), and generates HTML files in /home/ga/bed_cards/ directory with the naming pattern [LastName]_[MRN].html. All test data is synthetic: doctor names are fictional characters from House MD TV series (Dr. Gregory House, Dr. Lisa Cuddy, Dr. James Wilson), test patient is named 'TESTPATIENT' with MRN 'MRN99999', and test doctor ID 'DOC202' maps to 'Dr. Lisa Cuddy' in the database. The task verifies that the channel successfully performs database enrichment by looking up and including the doctor's full name (Dr. Lisa Cuddy) from the 'doctors' table using the doctor ID (DOC202) extracted from the HL7 message field PV1-7.1.
